content_en:
Ibn Katheer maintains: As-Sideeq, may Allah be pleased with him, knew best of all of the Prophet 's Companions about the Quran, because the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, ordered him to lead the other Companions in prayer, and he is reported to have said, "Let the one who recites the Quran best lead the people in Salaah.

*Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased wit her, reports that the

Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "It is not fitting for people among whom is Abu Bakr to be led by anyone other than him. " (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi.)

Abu Al-Qaasim Al-Baghawi quotes Maymoon Ibn Mahraan to have said: "When Abu Bakr was asked to judge in a certain case, he would resort to the Glorious Quran: if he did not find guidance, he would resort to the Sunnah of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him. If he again failed to find a solution, he would go out and ask the Muslims, "I am supposed to judge in so and so, does anyone of you remember the Prophet to have said anything concerning this case?" It used to happen that a group of people would come to him, all of whom remembered a judgment of the Prophet, in which case Abu Bakr would say, ‘All praise and thanks are due to Allah, Who has made among us people who memorize the Sayings of the Prophet. If there was one who could recall a judgment of the Prophet in a case in hand, Abu Bakr would gather the people and consult them as regards the case, and if they reached a consensus he would act accordingly. “Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, used to do the same. If he failed to find guidance in the Quran or Sunnah, he would ask if Abu Bakr had passed a judgment concerning the case in question, in which case he would resort to the judgment of Abu Bakr. But if he failed to find a judgment passed by Abu Bakr, he would gather the Muslims with a view to reaching unanimity on a certain judgment.

Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, was known as the foremost genealogist of Quraysh and the best of them at interpreting dreams. He used to interpret dreams during the lifetime of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him. According to Muhammad Ibn Sireen, he was the best at interpreting dreams, after the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him.

Abu Bakr, Ibn Katheer states, was the most eloquent speaker. Az-Zubayr Ibn Bakkaar is quoted to have said, “I heard some scholars say that the most eloquent speakers among the Prophet's Companions are Abu Bakr As-Sideeq and * Ali Ibn Abi Taalib, may Allah be pleased with them both. * Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab is reported to have said about Abu Bakr, "He is the most knowledgeable and most conscious of Allah,"

“Amr [bn Al-~Aas, may Allah be pleased with him, quotes the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, to have said, "Gabriel has come to me and said, ‘Allah orders you to consult Abu Bakr.' " (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal, may Allah be pleased with him narrates: When the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, wanted to send me to Yemen, he consulted some of his Companions, among whom were Abu Bakr, * Umar, ~ Uthmaan, “Ali, Talhah, Az-Zubayr, Usayd Ibn Hudayr. Every one of them stated his own opinion. Then the Prophet asked me, "What do you see, O Mu*aadh?" I replied, "I go with what Abu Bakr has said." To this the Prophet said, “Allah hates in His Heaven that the view of Abu Bakr be considered wrong." (Reported by At-Tabaraani, Abu Nu‘ aym and others.)

Abu Bakr's Steadfastness upon the Death of the Prophet:

As the Prophet's mission was completed, the time came for him to depart to his heavenly abode. He began to suffer from illness and his health deteriorated at an alarming speed.

On Monday 29th of Safar, 11 AH, the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, went to Al-Baqee*, prayed for the dead, and then returned to the house of his wife Maymoonah,

may Allah be pleased with her. The fever grew violent and the pain increased considerably. But, withstanding all this suffering in remarkable calmness and serenity, the Prophet led the prayer in the mosque. But he became too week and his illness worsened. He said to his wives: "Pour over me seven skins of water from different wells so that | may go out to the men and exhort them." They had him sit down ina tub belonging to Hafsah and poured water over him until he cried, "Enough, enough!" Then they helped him get dressed, bound his head, and ~Ali and *Abbaas supported him to the mosque. where he sat on the pulpit and addressed the people. He said: “There is a slave whom Almighty Allah gave the choice between this world and that which is with Him, and the slave has chosen that which is with Allah." It was only Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, may Allah be pleased with him, who perceived what the Prophet meant by the choice of words. He began to cry as he knew that the Prophet was referring to himself and that the choice meant imminent death. The Prophet realized that his intimate friend, Abu Bakr, had understood him, and asked him not to cry. He said: "The most bountiful of men to me in his companionship and his sacrifice of his wealth is Abu Bakr. If I were to choose a bosom friend, I would choose Abu Bakr — but companionship and brotherhood in faith will remain between us until Allah unites us in His presence."

Al-Bukhari narrates that on the early days of Safar, 11 AH. The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, went to the graves of the martyrs of Uhud and prayed for them. Then he stood on the pulpit and addressed his people: "1 am to precede you and 1 am your witness. Your trust with me is at the Fount4,

which verity I behold from her where now I stand. | have been

This is the Fount (of abundance) which Allah mentions in Surat, "“Al-Kawthar".

On the Day of Judgment, righteous Muslims will be allowed to drink from this Fount, aller which they will not grow thirsty ever again,

given the keys of worldly treasures. By Allah, I do not fear for you that you will associate partners with Allah after I pass away, but I do fear for you from this world, lest you should strike one another's necks for the acquisition of worldly gains.”

It is also narrated that when the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, went to Al-Baqee* cemetery, he prayed for forgiveness for the martyrs, saying, "Peace be on you, people of the graves! Happy are you as you are so much better than men who are alive. Dissension has come like waves of the darkest nights one after the other, each worse than the previous one."

*Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, narrates: When the Prophet , peace and blessings be upon him, grew seriously ill, he ordered the people to tell Abu Bakr to lead them in Salaah. But ~ Aa'ishah feared that it would greatly pain her father to take the place of the Prophet. So she said, "Messenger of Allah, Abu Bakr is a very sensitive man, not strong of voice and much given to weeping when he recites the Quran." To this, the Prophet answered, "You are even like the women that were with Joseph. Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in Salaah."

The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, lay much of the time with his head resting on * Aa'ishah's chest or lap. While bedridden and suffering severe pain, he called his daughter, Faatimah, and told her to sit beside him. Then he spoke to her in secret; she wept bitterly. When he found her overcome with grief, he whispered another secret to her, and this time she laughed. * Aa'ishah asked her about the secret that made her cry, but Faatimah refused to tell her, saying: "I am not going to disclose the secret of Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him.” When the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, passed away, ~ Aa'ishah said to her:

"I adjure you by the right that ] have upon you that you should narrate to me what the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said to you.” She said: "Well, now I can tell you. The first time, the Prophet , peace and blessings be upon him, told me that he would not recover from his fatal illness and therefore I wept. The second time he told me that I would be the first of his family to join him (in Paradise), and that made me laugh."

Anas Ibn Malik, may Allah be pleased with him, also narrates: When the ailment of the Prophet aggravated, he became unconscious and then Faatimah said, "Oh, How distressed my father is!" Whereupon the Prophet said, "Your father will have no more distress after today.”

The malady of the Prophet took a turn for the worse. His fever rose so high that his body could hardly be touched from the burning heat. His body was aching with pain, but he was too busy calling people to righteousness. Turning to his wives, who sat close by, he said: "O Faatimah, my daughter and you, Safiyyah, my aunt! Strive to do that which shall please Allah, for verily, I have no power with Him to save you in any

On the 11" of Rabee’ Al-Awwal, the fever and pain had somewhat abated and he recovered slightly, regaining a little strength. The Prophet, hearing the call to prayer, decided to go to the mosque. The prayer had already begun when he entered and Muslims were almost distracted from the joy at seeing the Prophet, peace and_ blessings be upon him. But Allah's Messenger motioned them to continue. Abu Bakr had been conscious of the stir behind him, and he realized that the Prophet must have entered the mosque. Without turning his head, Abu Bakr stepped back, but the Prophet placed his hand on his shoulder and pushed him forward gently as a sign that he should continue leading Muslims in Salaah. When he finished,

the Prophet sat on the pulpit and addressed his devoted Companions: "By Allah, I have not made anything lawful except that which Allah has declared lawful, nor have I declared anything unlawful except that which Allah has deemed unlawful,"

The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, seemed exhausted, so he returned to * Aa'ishah's house accompanied by two of his Companions. As the day advanced, his health failed rapidly. ~ Aa'ishah, seeing he was too frail, raised his head from the pillow and placed it on her lap. She kept moistening his forehead with a damp cloth. The fever was very high. But the noble Prophet remained calm and never complained. He had resigned to the will of Allah and repeated the following words over and over again: "In the company of those on whom is the Grace of Allah."
content_id:
Ibnu Katsir menyatakan: As-Sideeq, semoga Allah meridainya, adalah yang paling mengetahui tentang Al-Quran di antara seluruh Sahabat Nabi, karena Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memerintahkannya untuk mengimami para Sahabat lainnya dalam salah, dan beliau diriwayatkan bersabda, "Hendaklah orang yang paling baik membaca Al-Quran yang mengimami manusia dalam salah."

'Aisyah, semoga Allah meridainya, meriwayatkan bahwa

Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Tidaklah patut bagi suatu kaum yang di antara mereka terdapat Abu Bakr untuk diimami oleh selain dirinya." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi.)

Abu Al-Qasim Al-Baghawi mengutip Maymun bin Mahran yang berkata: "Ketika Abu Bakr diminta untuk memutuskan suatu perkara, ia akan merujuk kepada Al-Quran; jika tidak menemukan petunjuk di dalamnya, ia akan merujuk kepada Sunnah Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Jika kembali tidak menemukan penyelesaian, ia akan keluar dan bertanya kepada kaum muslimin, 'Aku harus memutuskan perkara ini dan itu, adakah di antara kalian yang mengingat Nabi pernah bersabda sesuatu tentang perkara ini?' Sering terjadi sekelompok orang datang kepadanya, semuanya mengingat suatu keputusan Nabi, dalam hal ini Abu Bakr akan berkata, 'Segala puji dan syukur hanya milik Allah, yang telah menjadikan di antara kami orang-orang yang menghafal sabda-sabda Nabi.' Jika ada seseorang yang dapat mengingat keputusan Nabi dalam suatu perkara, Abu Bakr akan mengumpulkan orang-orang dan bermusyawarah dengan mereka mengenai perkara tersebut, dan jika mereka mencapai kesepakatan, ia akan bertindak sesuai dengan kesepakatan itu. 'Umar, semoga Allah meridainya, biasa melakukan hal yang sama. Jika ia tidak menemukan petunjuk dalam Al-Quran atau Sunnah, ia akan bertanya apakah Abu Bakr pernah memberikan keputusan mengenai perkara yang bersangkutan, dalam hal ini ia akan mengikuti keputusan Abu Bakr. Namun jika ia tidak menemukan keputusan Abu Bakr, ia akan mengumpulkan kaum muslimin untuk mencapai kesepakatan atas suatu keputusan.

Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridainya, dikenal sebagai ahli silsilah terkemuka di kalangan Quraisy dan yang paling pandai di antara mereka dalam menafsirkan mimpi. Ia biasa menafsirkan mimpi semasa hidup Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Menurut Muhammad bin Sirin, ia adalah penafsir mimpi terbaik setelah Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam.

Abu Bakr, kata Ibnu Katsir, adalah orator paling fasih. Az-Zubair bin Bakkar dikutip berkata, "Aku mendengar beberapa ulama berkata bahwa orator paling fasih di antara para Sahabat Nabi adalah Abu Bakr As-Sideeq dan 'Ali bin Abi Talib, semoga Allah meridai keduanya." 'Umar bin Al-Khattab diriwayatkan berkata tentang Abu Bakr, "Dia adalah yang paling berilmu dan paling bertaqwa kepada Allah."

'Amr bin Al-'Ash, semoga Allah meridainya, mengutip Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Jibril telah datang kepadaku dan berkata, 'Allah memerintahkanmu untuk bermusyawarah dengan Abu Bakr.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Mu'adz bin Jabal, semoga Allah meridainya, meriwayatkan: Ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, hendak mengutusku ke Yaman, beliau bermusyawarah dengan beberapa Sahabatnya, di antaranya Abu Bakr, 'Umar, 'Utsman, 'Ali, Thalhah, Az-Zubair, dan Usaid bin Hudair. Masing-masing menyampaikan pendapatnya. Kemudian Nabi bertanya kepadaku, "Bagaimana pendapatmu, wahai Mu'adz?" Aku menjawab, "Aku sependapat dengan apa yang dikatakan Abu Bakr." Maka Nabi bersabda, "Allah membenci di langit-Nya jika pendapat Abu Bakr dianggap salah." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabarani, Abu Nu'aim, dan lainnya.)

Keteguhan Abu Bakr Saat Wafatnya Nabi:

Setelah misi Nabi sempurna, tibalah waktunya bagi beliau untuk berangkat menuju tempat peristirahatannya yang kekal. Beliau mulai menderita sakit dan kesehatannya memburuk dengan sangat cepat.

Pada hari Senin tanggal 29 Safar 11 H, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, pergi ke Al-Baqi', mendoakan para syuhada, kemudian kembali ke rumah istrinya Maimunah,

semoga Allah meridainya. Demam semakin tinggi dan rasa sakit bertambah hebat. Namun, dengan menanggung semua penderitaan itu dalam ketenangan dan kekhusyukan yang luar biasa, Nabi tetap mengimami salah di masjid. Tetapi beliau semakin lemah dan sakitnya bertambah parah. Beliau berkata kepada istri-istrinya: "Siramlah aku dengan tujuh geriba air dari sumur-sumur yang berbeda agar aku dapat keluar menemui orang-orang dan menasihati mereka." Mereka mendudukkannya dalam bejana milik Hafshah dan menyiramkan air ke atasnya hingga beliau berseru, "Cukup, cukup!" Kemudian mereka membantunya berpakaian, mengikat kepalanya, lalu 'Ali dan 'Abbas menopangnya ke masjid, di mana beliau duduk di atas mimbar dan berkhutbah kepada orang-orang. Beliau bersabda: "Ada seorang hamba yang Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala beri pilihan antara dunia ini dan apa yang ada di sisi-Nya, dan hamba itu telah memilih apa yang ada di sisi Allah." Hanya Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, semoga Allah meridainya, yang memahami apa yang dimaksud Nabi dengan pilihan kata tersebut. Ia mulai menangis karena ia tahu bahwa Nabi sedang merujuk kepada dirinya sendiri dan bahwa pilihan itu berarti kematian yang sudah dekat. Nabi menyadari bahwa sahabat karibnya, Abu Bakr, telah memahaminya, dan memintanya untuk tidak menangis. Beliau bersabda: "Orang yang paling dermawan kepadaku dalam persahabatan dan pengorbanan hartanya adalah Abu Bakr. Seandainya aku boleh mengambil seorang khalil (sahabat karib), niscaya aku akan memilih Abu Bakr — tetapi persahabatan dan persaudaraan dalam iman akan tetap ada di antara kita sampai Allah menyatukan kita di hadirat-Nya."

Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa pada hari-hari awal bulan Safar 11 H, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, pergi ke kuburan para syuhada Uhud dan mendoakan mereka. Kemudian beliau berdiri di atas mimbar dan berkhutbah kepada umatnya: "Aku mendahului kalian dan aku adalah saksi atas kalian. Perjumpaan kalian denganku adalah di al-Haudh (Telaga),

yang sungguh aku lihat dari tempatku berdiri sekarang ini. Aku telah

Ini adalah al-Haudh (Telaga kelimpahan) yang Allah sebutkan dalam Surat Al-Kautsar.

Pada Hari Kiamat, kaum muslimin yang saleh akan diperkenankan minum dari Telaga ini, setelah itu mereka tidak akan pernah merasa haus lagi.

diberi kunci-kunci perbendaharaan dunia. Demi Allah, aku tidak khawatir kalian akan mempersekutukan Allah sepeninggalku, tetapi yang aku khawatirkan adalah dunia ini, agar kalian tidak saling membunuh demi memperoleh keuntungan duniawi."

Diriwayatkan pula bahwa ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, pergi ke pemakaman Al-Baqi', beliau memohonkan ampunan bagi para syuhada, seraya bersabda, "Semoga keselamatan atas kalian, wahai penghuni kubur! Beruntunglah kalian karena kalian jauh lebih baik daripada orang-orang yang masih hidup. Fitnah telah datang bagaikan gelombang malam yang paling gelap, satu demi satu, masing-masing lebih buruk dari yang sebelumnya."

'Aisyah, semoga Allah meridainya, meriwayatkan: Ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, sakit parah, beliau memerintahkan agar orang-orang menyampaikan kepada Abu Bakr untuk mengimami mereka dalam salah. Tetapi 'Aisyah khawatir hal itu akan sangat menyedihkan ayahnya jika harus menggantikan posisi Nabi. Maka ia berkata, "Ya Rasulullah, Abu Bakr adalah seorang yang sangat perasa, suaranya tidak kuat, dan banyak menangis ketika membaca Al-Quran." Nabi menjawab, "Kalian sama seperti wanita-wanita yang bersama Yusuf. Perintahkan Abu Bakr untuk mengimami orang-orang dalam salah."

Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, berbaring sepanjang waktu dengan kepalanya bersandar di dada atau pangkuan 'Aisyah. Saat terbaring sakit dan menderita kesakitan yang hebat, beliau memanggil putrinya, Fatimah, dan memintanya duduk di sampingnya. Kemudian beliau berbisik kepadanya secara rahasia; Fatimah menangis tersedu-sedu. Ketika beliau mendapatinya diliputi kesedihan, beliau membisikkan rahasia lain kepadanya, dan kali ini Fatimah tersenyum. 'Aisyah bertanya kepadanya tentang rahasia yang membuatnya menangis, tetapi Fatimah menolak memberitahunya, seraya berkata: "Aku tidak akan membocorkan rahasia Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam." Setelah Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, wafat, 'Aisyah berkata kepadanya:

"Aku memintamu demi hak yang aku miliki atasmu agar engkau menceritakan kepadaku apa yang Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, katakan kepadamu." Fatimah berkata: "Baiklah, sekarang aku bisa memberitahumu. Pertama kali, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memberitahuku bahwa beliau tidak akan sembuh dari sakitnya yang berat ini, maka aku pun menangis. Kedua kalinya, beliau memberitahuku bahwa aku akan menjadi anggota keluarganya yang pertama menyusulnya (di surga), dan itulah yang membuatku tersenyum."

Anas Ibn Malik, semoga Allah meridhainya, juga meriwayatkan: Ketika penyakit Nabi semakin parah, beliau tidak sadarkan diri, lalu Faatimah berkata, "Oh, betapa menderitanya ayahku!" Maka Nabi bersabda, "Ayahmu tidak akan merasakan penderitaan lagi setelah hari ini."

Penyakit Nabi semakin memburuk. Demamnya naik begitu tinggi sehingga tubuhnya hampir tidak dapat disentuh karena panasnya yang membakar. Tubuhnya kesakitan, tetapi beliau terlalu sibuk menyeru manusia kepada kebenaran. Menoleh kepada istri-istrinya yang duduk di dekatnya, beliau bersabda: "Wahai Faatimah, putriku, dan engkau, Safiyyah, bibiku! Berusahalah melakukan apa yang diridhai Allah, karena sesungguhnya aku tidak memiliki kuasa apa pun di sisi-Nya untuk menyelamatkan kalian."

Pada tanggal 11 Rabee' Al-Awwal, demam dan rasa sakit agak mereda dan beliau sedikit pulih, mendapatkan kembali sedikit kekuatan. Nabi, mendengar panggilan salah, memutuskan untuk pergi ke masjid. Salah telah dimulai ketika beliau masuk, dan kaum Muslim hampir teralihkan karena kegembiraan melihat Nabi, semoga Allah melimpahkan shalawat dan salam kepadanya. Namun Rasulullah memberi isyarat kepada mereka untuk melanjutkan. Abu Bakr menyadari adanya kegaduhan di belakangnya, dan ia mengerti bahwa Nabi pasti telah memasuki masjid. Tanpa menolehkan kepalanya, Abu Bakr mundur selangkah, tetapi Nabi meletakkan tangannya di bahunya dan mendorongnya maju dengan lembut sebagai isyarat agar ia terus memimpin kaum Muslim dalam salah. Ketika selesai,

Nabi duduk di atas mimbar dan berkhutbah kepada para Sahabatnya yang setia: "Demi Allah, aku tidak menghalalkan sesuatu kecuali apa yang Allah nyatakan halal, dan aku tidak mengharamkan sesuatu kecuali apa yang Allah nyatakan haram."

Nabi, semoga Allah melimpahkan shalawat dan salam kepadanya, tampak kelelahan, maka beliau kembali ke rumah 'Aa'ishah ditemani dua orang Sahabatnya. Seiring berjalannya hari, kesehatannya memburuk dengan cepat. 'Aa'ishah, melihat beliau terlalu lemah, mengangkat kepala beliau dari bantal dan meletakkannya di pangkuannya. Ia terus membasahi dahi beliau dengan kain basah. Demamnya sangat tinggi. Namun Nabi yang mulia tetap tenang dan tidak pernah mengeluh. Beliau telah berserah diri kepada kehendak Allah dan mengulang-ulang kata-kata berikut: "Bersama mereka yang mendapat karunia Allah."
content_ar:
من أبرز صفات أبي بكر الصديق رضي الله عنه علمه الواسع وفقهه العميق في الشريعة والسياسة. قال ابن كثير: «كان أبو بكر أعلم الصحابة بأنساب العرب وأخبارهم وأشعارهم، وكان أعرفهم بمحاسنهم ومساوئهم، وكان من أوسع الصحابة علماً بالكتاب والسنة». وقد كان يُستفتى في المسائل الدقيقة وكانت فتاواه قاطعةً في الغالب.

وقد دلَّ على عمق فقهه قوله لمَّا وجد الصحابة مضطربين حين وفاة النبي ﷺ: «من كان يعبد محمداً فإن محمداً قد مات، ومن كان يعبد الله فإن الله حيٌّ لا يموت». وهذه الكلمة الحاسمة ردَّت الاطمئنان إلى نفوس الصحابة وثبَّتتهم على الإيمان في ساعة الأزمة الكبرى.

وكذلك دلَّ على فقهه السياسي الناضج موقفه الحازم من مانعي الزكاة حين أصرَّ على قتالهم وقال: «والله لو منعوني عناقاً كانوا يؤدُّونها إلى رسول الله ﷺ لقاتلتهم على منعها». وقد كان عمر في البداية متردِّداً ثم تبيَّن له أن أبا بكر كان على صواب. فأبو بكر رضي الله عنه كان يُقدِّم النصَّ الشرعي على الرأي والمصلحة الآنية.
